mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing required returns and driving immediate positive price adjustments in the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance or negative mean returns during periods of falling FEDFUNDS over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate negative repricing of equity earnings over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4pp, invalidating the negative earnings revision channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, generating sustained revenue growth expectations that drive positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of historical observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate profit margins, causing rapid negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high inflation periods yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, contradicting the margin compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate confirm a restrictive monetary stance that erodes real discount rates and future cash flow valuations, producing negative equity returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and non-falling FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with forward returns exceeding zero in more than 65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the rank information coefficient falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product signals an economic contraction that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings forecasts, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 declines, invalidating the earnings contraction channel.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a monetary easing cycle indicates improving supply-side efficiency and lower input costs, which boosts corporate margins and drives rapid positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during easing periods fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
